name: Claude Talk to Figma MCP
what_it_does: Enables AI agents to read, analyze, and modify Figma designs via MCP. Connects through a local WebSocket server and a Figma plugin, allowing agents to extract document info, create shapes/text/frames, modify styles (colors, borders, shadows, typography, auto-layout), and work with components.
use_cases: AI-assisted UI design iteration directly in Figma
Programmatic creation of design elements from natural language descriptions
Auditing and analyzing existing Figma designs for consistency
Batch modifications to colors, typography, and layout across a design file
Extracting design tokens and component information for handoff
not_for: Figma-to-code generation (planned but not yet available)
Collaborative real-time design with multiple human designers simultaneously
Working with Figma files without Figma Desktop installed
best_when: You want an AI agent to directly read and manipulate Figma designs without requiring Figma Dev Mode, and you are comfortable running a local WebSocket server.
avoid_when: You need a cloud-hosted or headless solution without Figma Desktop, or you need production-grade stability (still v0.9.x).
alternatives: Figma REST API (manual integration, no MCP)

auth.notes: Uses a WebSocket channel ID copied from the Figma plugin to establish a connection. No API keys or OAuth needed.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed. Requires Figma account (free or paid) and Figma Desktop.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No specific documentation on error handling or error message formats.
known_agent_gotchas: Requires Figma Desktop running with the plugin active - cannot work headlessly
WebSocket channel ID must be manually copied and provided to the agent each session
Still pre-1.0; API surface may change between versions

security_score_components.security_notes: Figma design tool MCP. Figma OAuth2 token. Design files may contain proprietary IP. Read vs edit scope — use read-only for analysis agents.
